user_query:
You're my project coordinator for a small operational improvement sprint. Read input/project_brief.json to understand the scope, constraints, and acceptance criteria. Then do the following:

1) Create a realistic, time-bounded plan and save it to output/plan.json. The JSON must include: (a) a top-level "tasks" array of 3+ items where each task has "title" (string), "estimate" (string), "dependencies" (array), and "done" (boolean); and (b) a top-level "next_steps" array of strings.

2) Maintain a persistent task tracker at memory/tasks.md for this engagement: record a Task ID (you choose a stable ID), a one-line description, status updates at start, during progress, and at completion, timestamps for "Requested" and "Updated", and any background process details (session ID, PID, server, command) if you start anything in the background. Keep it concise (max 50 lines and 2KB). Update the tracker BEFORE you report status in chat.

3) Export a snapshot of the current tracker entry to output/task_state_snapshot.md using the following exact field labels (one per line):
- Task ID: ...
- Description: ...
- Status: In-Progress | Completed | Failed | Paused
- Requested: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM (local or UTC, specify)
- Updated: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM (local or UTC, specify)
- Background: session-id (PID) on server-name — command   OR   Background: none
- Notes: ...
- Result: ...
Ensure this snapshot stays within 50 lines and 2KB.

4) Produce output/status_report.md that summarizes: (a) what you did, (b) at least two distinct "Progress Update" sections with timestamps, and (c) a final "Outcome" and "Next Steps" section.

5) Append a machine-readable progress log to output/progress_log.jsonl with at least two lines; each line must be a JSON object containing at minimum "timestamp" (ISO8601) and "event" (string).

At the end, mark the task status as Completed in memory/tasks.md and reflect that in the snapshot. Use only relative paths (input/, output/, memory/).
